statement.markdown: Given a nearly sorted array, where the array is sorted in ascending order except for a small number of elements, write a function to sort the array in ascending order using bubble sort algorithm.
statement.inputFormat: Input will be in the form of an array of integers.
statement.outputFormat: Output will be the sorted array in ascending order.
statement.notes: The array is nearly sorted, meaning that the elements are in a sorted order except for a small number of elements.

hints.text: Check if the array is already sorted before applying the bubble sort algorithm.

editorial.title: How to Fix the BUG!
editorial.intuition: The bubble sort algorithm should be modified to handle nearly sorted arrays.
editorial.bruteForce: The bubble sort algorithm is not efficient for nearly sorted arrays.
editorial.betterSolution: Use a modified bubble sort algorithm to sort the array.
editorial.optimalSolution: In the modified bubble sort algorithm, check if the array is already sorted before applying the bubble sort algorithm.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The modified bubble sort algorithm is correct because it handles nearly sorted arrays efficiently.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run of the modified bubble sort algorithm.
commonMistakes: Not checking if the array is already sorted before applying the bubble sort algorithm.
